title: Bumble Introduces an AI Dating Assistant, Bee
text: Bumble has introduced Bee, a generative AI assistant designed to serve as a personal matchmaker. The tool learns about users' values, relationship goals, communication style, lifestyle, and dating intentions through private conversations, then recommends compatible matches. The AI assistant currently powers a new experience called Dates, which identifies two users with shared intentions and goals, notifying both with explanations of their compatibility. Bee operates through conversational interactions—typing and speaking—similar to other AI chatbots. Bee is currently in internal pilot testing, with a beta launch coming soon. Beyond matchmaking, the company envisions Bee expanding into date suggestions and gathering anonymous feedback from previous matches. As part of a larger AI-focused overhaul, Bumble is experimenting with removing its signature swipe mechanism in select markets. The company plans to introduce chapter-based profiles and dynamic ways for somebody to express interest beyond traditional swiping. CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd explained the vision: this is going to drive more dynamic engagement, spark better conversation, and ultimately drive better KPIs across the board. Bumble reported strong Q4 earnings with $224.2 million in revenue and 7.9% growth in average revenue per paying user, reaching $22.20. Stock prices surged approximately 40% following the announcement.
